name: Castillo de la Encomienda de Víboras - circular from las Casillas
name_original: Castillo de la Encomienda de Víboras - circular desde las Casillas
region_intro: Hiking around Martos reveals a landscape characterized by extensive olive groves, often referred to as a "sea of olive trees," and the dramatic limestone peak of Cerro de la Peña. The town is situated at the western end of the Sierra Jabalcuz, contributing to varied terrain. This region offers a blend of agricultural scenery, historic sites, and natural features for outdoor exploration. Best hiking trails around Martos The most popular hiking route is Víboras Reservoir Loop Trail, a 8.9 miles (14.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 48 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic views of the reservoir and surrounding olive groves. Another top favourite among local hikers is Castillo de la Peña - circular from Martos, a moderate 3.5 miles (5.6 km) path. This trail leads to the historic fortress remains, providing panoramic views of the area. Local hikers also love the The olive groves of Torredonjimeno - circular via the green way of Oil, a 9.2 miles (14.8 km) trail leading through extensive olive groves along the Vía Verde del Aceite, often completed in about 4 hours 2 minutes. Hiking around Martos is defined by vast olive groves, limestone peaks, and the Sierra Jabalcuz mountain range. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging ascents. The routes in Martos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Martos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Martos?
A: There are over 60 hiking trails available around Martos, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include routes through extensive olive groves, up limestone peaks, and into the Sierra Jabalcuz mountain range.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Martos?
A: Hikes around Martos feature varied terrain, from the vast 'sea of olive trees' to the dramatic limestone peak of Cerro de la Peña. You'll find paths winding through agricultural landscapes, alongside reservoirs, and into the foothills of the Sierra Jabalcuz, offering both gentle slopes and more challenging ascents.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, Martos offers several easy hiking options. Approximately 20 of the available routes are classified as easy, providing accessible paths suitable for beginners or those looking for a relaxed stroll through the scenic olive groves.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see while hiking near Martos?
A: Many trails in Martos lead to or pass by significant historical sites. You can explore the remains of the &lt;strong&gt;Castillo de la Peña&lt;/strong&gt;, offering panoramic views, or encounter the &lt;strong&gt;Torre del Homenaje&lt;/strong&gt;, a remnant of the old city walls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987825473/the-olive-groves-of-torredonjimeno-circular-via-the-green-way-of-oil" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The olive groves of Torredonjimeno - circular via the green way of Oil&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, follows the historic Vía Verde del Aceite, where you might spot the impressive Viaduct of the Piedra del Águila Stream.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Martos?
A: Yes, Martos has several excellent circular hiking routes. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987249934/viboras-reservoir-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Víboras Reservoir Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic views of the water, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987237527/castillo-de-la-pena-circular-from-martos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de la Peña - circular from Martos&lt;/a&gt;, leading to the historic fortress remains.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Martos?
A: The trails in Martos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 200 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning vistas of olive groves, the dramatic limestone peaks, and the blend of natural beauty with historical landmarks.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking trails in Martos?
A: Martos offers several family-friendly hiking trails, particularly those with easier difficulty ratings and accessible paths. Routes that explore the olive groves or offer gentle ascents to viewpoints are often suitable for families looking for a pleasant outdoor experience.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails around Martos?
A: Many hiking trails in Martos are suitable for dogs, especially those that traverse open landscapes like the olive groves. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, particularly near agricultural areas or wildlife, and to carry water for both yourself and your pet.

Q: What are some notable natural features or viewpoints to discover?
A: The region is dominated by the 'sea of olive trees' and the striking &lt;strong&gt;Cerro de la Peña&lt;/strong&gt;, which offers incredible panoramic views. You can also explore the varied terrain of the Sierra Jabalcuz. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2876708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Tambor Summit (1075 m)&lt;/a&gt; is another prominent natural feature providing expansive vistas.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Martos?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in and around Martos, especially near popular trailheads or starting points within the town. For routes that begin further afield, it's advisable to check specific route details for designated parking areas.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Martos?
A: The best time to hike in Martos is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant for outdoor activities, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, sometimes wetter, winter months.
tips: Medieval fortification next to the Víboras River located in the town of Las Casillas, in the municipality of Martos. It has its origin in the construction of the 9th century
